data.title: Atos acknowledged as top in IT/OT cybersecurity services by PAC in 2025. France's PAC Innovation Radar report.
data.description: In 2025, Atos was acknowledged as a top performer in IT/OT cybersecurity services in France by PAC Innovation Radar. Their exemplary services and expertise in this field propelled them to be recognized as the best in class, highlighting their commitment to providing exceptional cybersecurity solutions. This recognition emphasizes Atos's leading role in the industry, showcasing their dedication to delivering top-notch IT/OT cybersecurity services.

data.title: Wix's Board approves $2 billion share repurchase program over 2 years (FY2026-2027) for ordinary shares and/or convertible notes.
data.description: Wix.com Ltd., under the ticker symbol NASDAQ: WIX, declared a new investment plan today. The company's Board of Directors sanctioned a 2-year initiative, running from fiscal years 2026 to 2027, aimed at buying back its securities. Wix has allocated funds of up to $2 billion for repurchasing its own ordinary shares and/or convertible notes through this program.

data.title: Britebound and Jamie Oliver partner to provide food career opportunities for US youth.
data.description: In Boston on January 28, 2026, Britebound™, formerly known as American Student Assistance® (ASA), a nonprofit organization focused on reshaping career exploration and post-high school planning for youth, revealed a collaboration with Jamie Oliver, renowned British chef and restaurateur. This partnership aims to enhance opportunities for young individuals to explore career options and achieve success after high school.

data.title: Funding Circle receives Silver Fair Payment Code accreditation, reinforcing support for UK small businesses.
data.description: A prestigious recognition has been awarded to Funding Circle, the top SME finance platform in the UK. They have proudly achieved the Silver accreditation for the Fair Payment Code.

data.title: UnitedHealth plans to target $1 billion in cost savings by 2026 with AI, focusing on productivity. Leading in AI for healthcare industry.
data.description: UnitedHealth Group Inc. (UNH) is venturing into AI with the goal of achieving substantial cost savings and improved efficiency starting this year. CEO Stephen Hemsley disclosed during the fourth-quarter earnings call that the company aims to play a strategic role in the increasing adoption of AI in the healthcare industry. The focus of UnitedHealth Group's AI strategy is on enhancing productivity, automating processes, and revamping core service operations. CEO Timothy Noel stated that the company foresees operating cost cuts of around $1 billion in 2026, with a significant portion attributed to AI implementations. UnitedHealth Group has already witnessed positive outcomes from its AI initiatives, especially in customer service operations. Hemsley and Noel emphasized the company's commitment to becoming a frontrunner in leveraging AI technologies to drive operational efficiencies and cost savings.

data.title: Abu Dhabi Customs partners with CrimsonLogic to develop AI-enabled Integrated Customs Operations System (ICOS).
data.description: Abu Dhabi Customs (ADC) in UAE revealed a new partnership with CrimsonLogic, a subsidiary of PSA International, on Jan. 28, 2026. This collaboration aims to create the Integrated Customs Operations System (ICOS), which will leverage Artificial Intelligence (AI) technology. The system is designed to streamline customs operations and enhance efficiency. The partnership highlights the commitment to innovation and digitalization in customs operations.

data.title: Lassen Peak secures $10M in venture financing for weapons search tech, led by Structural Capital for product launch.
data.description: Bellevue, Washington - On January 28, 2026, Lassen Peak disclosed that they have successfully secured funding for product development and market entry. The investment, exceeding $10 million, came from a combination of debt and equity financings. Structural Capital, headquartered in Menlo, spearheaded the financings.

data.title: Lockheed Martin (NYSE: LMT) has a strong $179 billion backlog heading into Q4 earnings. Management's guidance hints at beating $5.87 EPS expectation with potential $6.56 EPS. Analysts cautious due to past execution issues.
data.description: Lockheed Martin Corp. will be reporting its fourth-quarter earnings on Jan. 29 with a $179 billion backlog. Analysts expect $5.87 EPS, but the company's guidance suggests potential for $6.56 EPS. This indicates a substantial upside surprise if they meet their targets. Despite previous execution stumbles, Lockheed Martin's management remains optimistic. Wall Street is cautious due to past issues, but the company's outlook points towards possible outperformance. The full article can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Record return of HK$331B in 2025 for Hong Kong Exchange Fund, achieving 8% investment return as total assets hit HK$4.15T amid global market rally.
data.description: The Exchange Fund managed by HKMA has reported a remarkable 8% return on investments, generating an unprecedented income of HK $331 billion due to the surge in global markets. The total assets under the fund have now soared to HK $4.15 trillion, reflecting the fund's successful investment strategies amidst the market rally.

data.title: Yutong Bus reports sales growth in 2025, focusing on sustainable public transportation worldwide. Symbol: SHA: 600066.
data.description: ZHENGZHOU, China - On January 28, 2026, Yutong Bus (SHA: 600066), a prominent global commercial vehicle manufacturer, showcased consistent operational strength, growth fueled by innovation, and an integrated approach to Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) factors. This has further solidified their position in value creation for the long term. The company's resilient operating performance has led to continuous growth and sustainability.

data.description: Company records its best-ever annual and quarterly performance, attributed to AI memory strength and premium products like HBM. data.title: Deere & Co invests $70 million in NC facility; Trump praises 'only excavator entirely made' in US.
data.description: President Donald Trump disclosed plans for a new John Deere facility under Deere & Co. in the US. The $70 million excavator manufacturing plant will be located in Kernersville, North Carolina, as announced during Trump's speech in Clive, Iowa. This facility aims to be the sole excavator produced entirely in the USA. In addition, John Deere confirmed the plan, along with the commencement of constructing a distribution center near Hebron, Indiana. Through the Kernersville plant, John Deere's manufacturing capabilities will be bolstered with the use of cutting-edge technologies to manufacture top-tier excavators for the construction industry. The facility will take over the production of next-gen excavators previously manufactured elsewhere.
